name: SRT1720 improved healthspan and mean lifespan in standard-diet mice
summary: A small-molecule sirtuin-activation signal supports mammalian tractability but remains broad and not human validated.
statement: Long-term SRT1720 supplementation in male mice fed a standard diet increased mean lifespan, improved healthspan, reduced age-associated metabolic risk markers, and lowered inflammatory gene-expression signals in liver and muscle.
population_or_model: Male C57BL/6J mice fed standard diet with or without SRT1720
time_horizon: Treatment from about 28 weeks of age for the remainder of life
quantitative_note: Mean lifespan increased 8.8%; the reported median lifespan trend of 5 weeks was not statistically significant, and 90th percentile survival did not differ.
caveats: SRT1720 acts through broad SIRT1/NAD-linked biology and is not a selective histone-only intervention.
The intervention was tested in mice, not humans.
Maximum lifespan was not increased in the standard-diet arm.
